disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms began within days of completing a 7-day course of broad-spectrum antibiotics for a respiratory infection
trigger_question_examples: Have you taken any medications recently, like antibiotics?
What were you treated with for the chest infection?
Did anything change with your health or medications just before this started?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, I just finished a week of antibiotics for a chest infection a few days ago — the strong broad kind. This started right after.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Thick white curd-like plaques over tongue and buccal mucosa with a cottony mouth sensation and loss of taste
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe what your mouth looks and feels like?
Tell me more about the white coating and your sense of taste.
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's thick and white, kind of curdy, all over my tongue and cheeks, and my mouth feels cottony and dry. I can't taste anything anymore.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Localised oral burning pain markedly worsened by acidic, spicy or physically rough foods
trigger_question_examples: What makes the pain better or worse?
Does anything in particular set off the burning when you eat or drink?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Anything acidic or spicy is agony — orange juice, anything with a bit of texture. Cool plain sips are the only thing I can manage.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient is restricting fluid and food intake because of oral pain, with a moderate dehydration risk compounded by living alone
trigger_question_examples: How much are you managing to drink and eat at the moment?
Are you able to keep fluids up, or is the pain stopping you?
Is there anyone at home who can help you if it gets worse?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Honestly I'm barely drinking because it hurts so much, and I haven't really eaten. I'm on my own so there's no one to keep an eye on me.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No deep retrosternal or central chest pain on swallowing — argues against oesophageal candidiasis extension at present
trigger_question_examples: When you swallow, do you get any pain deep in the centre of your chest?
Is the pain only in your mouth, or does it go down behind your breastbone?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing deep in my chest. The pain is all up in my mouth — swallowing the food past that is fine, it's just my mouth that burns.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I haven't really noticed anything like that.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Bilateral angular cheilitis — cracking and soreness at the oral commissures
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed anything around your lips or the corners of your mouth?
Are there any cracks or sores at the edges of your mouth?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, the corners of my mouth have gone cracked and sore over the last day or so.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fevers, and no difficulty swallowing solids down to the stomach (no dysphagia) — pertinent negatives for systemic or oesophageal involvement
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fevers or felt generally unwell?
Do you have any trouble getting food or drink to actually go down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fevers, I've checked. And swallowing itself is okay — it's just the burning in my mouth that stops me.
